Connect a database or start a fresh one. Edit data in grid, kanban, or gallery views. Build forms and share them with a link. A no-code database with no row limits or fees.

ideas.title: Runway Gen-3 text-to-video
ideas.hook: Want to make a cinematic video clip from one sentence? Here's how.
ideas.script: Go to runwayml.com and sign in for free. Click Try Gen-3 Alpha. Pick text to video at the top. Type a prompt like "drone shot flying over snowy mountains at sunrise." Set the duration to 5 seconds and hit generate. Wait about a minute. Download the clip and drop it straight into your edit. You just made a stock-quality shot with zero footage.

ideas.title: Quizgecko
ideas.hook: This AI turns any text or PDF into a quiz in seconds.
ideas.script: Go to quizgecko.com and paste text, a link, or a PDF. Pick the question type like multiple choice or short answer. It generates a full quiz from the content instantly. Take it yourself or share the link with classmates. Adjust the difficulty to match your level. Export the questions to study offline. Test yourself on anything you just read.

ideas.title: Brand Name Generator
ideas.hook: This prompt names your brand in 20 seconds.
ideas.script: Open ChatGPT. Type this: "Give me 20 brand name ideas for \[what you do\]. Mix short, brandable, and descriptive names, and check that each is easy to spell and say." It rapid fires names at you. Each is easy to remember. You pick a favorite and check if the domain is free. Naming used to take weeks. Now it takes a coffee break.
ideas.publisher: "Give me 20 brand name ideas for \[what you do\]. Mix short, brandable, and descriptive names, and check that each is easy to spell and say."

ideas.title: Free stock-style clips with Hailuo
ideas.hook: Generate stock footage that does not exist anywhere else.
ideas.script: Open hailuoai.video and sign in. Click Text to Video. Type the exact stock shot you need, like "coffee being poured in slow motion." Generate it on the free tier. Make a few angles. Download the clips. Custom stock footage no one else has, for free.

ideas.title: Chrome flags
ideas.hook: Chrome has an experimental settings page hidden from normal users.
ideas.script: In the address bar, type chrome colon slash slash flags and hit Enter. You will see hundreds of experimental features you can turn on. Search for "tab groups" or "reading list" to enable hidden tools. Toggle one to Enabled and relaunch the browser. Try one at a time so you know what changed. These are features Google is still testing. Power users unlock them early here.

ideas.title: Speechify for studying
ideas.hook: This AI reads my textbooks out loud so I can study while walking.
ideas.script: Install Speechify and import a PDF, article, or scanned page. Pick a natural-sounding voice you actually like. Hit play and it reads the whole document aloud. Speed it up to 2x to power through readings. Snap a photo of a physical page to listen to it. Study with your eyes off the screen. Turn dead time into study time.

ideas.title: Tana
ideas.hook: This is the AI notes app that thinks in objects, not pages.
ideas.script: Open Tana and create a node for a meeting. Tag it with a supertag so it inherits fields automatically. Type your notes underneath as bullet nodes. Trigger an AI command to extract tasks into their own nodes. Build a live view that pulls every task tagged this week. Now your notes are a database that organizes itself.

ideas.title: Connect Claude to your tools with MCP
ideas.hook: Here is the one config file that turns Claude into a power user.
ideas.script: Open claude\_desktop\_config.json from your app settings. Add an mcpServers object at the top level. Each tool is one entry with a command and arguments. Use npx servers for filesystem, GitHub, and more. Save the file and fully restart Claude Desktop. Look for the plug icon to confirm the servers connected. One file unlocks every integration at once.

ideas.title: Capacities
ideas.hook: This free notes app gives every idea its own profile, like a social network for your brain.
ideas.script: Open Capacities and create a new object for a book you are reading. Notice it has its own type with custom fields. Link it to a person object and a note object. Use the AI assistant to summarize a long highlight. Open the daily note and drop quick thoughts that link automatically. Your knowledge finally has structure without the busywork.

ideas.title: Notion AI writing
ideas.hook: Hit space in Notion and it writes the doc for you.
ideas.script: Open any Notion page and press the space bar. Type what you want like a project brief or weekly update. Notion AI drafts it inside the page. Highlight a messy paragraph and choose Improve writing. Use Summarize on long notes to get the key points. Ask it to turn bullet points into a full paragraph. Your notes become finished docs without leaving the page.

ideas.title: Surfer SEO
ideas.hook: This tool tells you exactly which words Google wants in your article.
ideas.script: Open Surfer and start a Content Editor. Enter your target keyword. It pulls the top ranking pages and builds a term list. Write in the editor and watch your score climb. Add the green checklist terms as you go. Hit the recommended word count. Publish a post built to rank.

ideas.title: Suno AI full song
ideas.hook: Make a full song with vocals from one line of text.
ideas.script: Go to Suno and sign up for free credits. Click Create and turn on Custom mode. Type your lyrics in the box, or let Suno write them. Add a style like "lofi hip hop, mellow, female vocals." Hit Create and wait 30 seconds. It returns two full tracks with vocals and instruments. Download the MP3 and you have an original song nobody else owns.

ideas.title: Notion AI
ideas.hook: Your messy meeting notes can turn into action items in one click, for free.
ideas.script: Open any Notion page with rough notes. Highlight the whole block. Hit space to bring up Notion AI. Type "summarize and pull out action items." Watch it rewrite the wall of text into a clean summary with a checklist. Now type "draft a follow-up email from this." It writes the email in seconds. Stop typing things twice and let your notes do the work.

ideas.title: Make Claude your meeting note taker
ideas.hook: Paste a messy meeting transcript and Claude returns clean action items.
ideas.script: Open Claude and paste your raw meeting transcript or notes. Type: "Turn this into a summary, a list of decisions, and action items with owners and due dates." Claude sorts the chaos into clear sections. The action items come tagged with who owns what. Ask "draft a follow-up email to the team from this." It writes it ready to send. Meeting done, paperwork done.
ideas.publisher: "Turn this into a summary, a list of decisions, and action items with owners and due dates."

ideas.title: Vaultwarden
ideas.hook: This free GitHub project runs a full Bitwarden password server in 30 seconds.
ideas.script: Password managers want a yearly fee for family sharing and 2FA. Vaultwarden is a free Bitwarden-compatible server you host yourself. Run docker run -d --name vaultwarden -p 80:80 vaultwarden/server. The official Bitwarden apps connect straight to your server. You get premium vault features unlocked for nothing. All your logins stay on hardware you control. Sync across every device with zero subscription. The cloud company never sees a single password.

ideas.title: Jasper
ideas.hook: I wrote a full marketing campaign in four minutes with one tool.
ideas.script: Open Jasper and click Create then pick the Campaign workflow. Type your product name and one sentence about who it is for. Choose your tone like bold or friendly. Jasper writes the landing page, three emails, and five ads at once. Edit any line by highlighting it and clicking Rephrase. Drop the ads straight into your ad manager. Hit export and you have a launch kit in minutes.

ideas.title: watchexec
ideas.hook: Run any command automatically when your files change.
ideas.script: watchexec watches your folder and reruns a command on every save. Perfect for tests, builds, and live reload. Install it with winget install watchexec.watchexec on Windows. On Mac run brew install watchexec. Run watchexec then your test command. Edit a file and it reruns instantly. It respects gitignore and debounces rapid changes. Your feedback loop just got automatic.
ideas.publisher: winget install watchexec.watchexec / brew install watchexec

ideas.title: Phidata agent with a UI
ideas.hook: I built an AI agent with a chat interface in under twenty lines.
ideas.script: Run pip install phidata openai. Create an Agent with a model and a description. Add the DuckDuckGo and YFinance tools for live data. Wrap it in a Playground app from phi.playground. Run the script and open the local URL. Chat with your agent in a clean web UI. Ask for a stock summary and it pulls real numbers. A polished agent demo in minutes.

ideas.title: Pipedream scheduled scraper to Notion
ideas.hook: This runs a scraper every morning and dumps it into Notion for free.
ideas.script: In Pipedream create a workflow with a Schedule trigger. Set it to run daily at your chosen time. Add a custom code step that fetches and parses a page. Add the Notion Create Page action below it. Map the scraped fields into the Notion properties. Deploy and it runs on its own every day. Your research database fills itself overnight.

ideas.title: Smartlead outreach at scale
ideas.hook: I ran a 1,000-lead cold email campaign from one dashboard.
ideas.script: Open Smartlead and add multiple sending mailboxes. Import your leads with a CSV. Spin out personalization with AI variables. Set up automatic follow-ups. Enable warmup to protect deliverability. Rotate inboxes so volume stays safe. Track opens and replies in real time. Enterprise outreach on a startup budget.

ideas.title: Postwise
ideas.hook: This tool writes tweets designed to get followers, not just likes.
ideas.script: Open Postwise and click Write. Pick a goal like growth or engagement. Type your topic and generate options. Use the Viral Tweet Generator for proven hooks. Build a thread with the thread tool. Schedule it with the Magic Schedule feature. Grow your account while you sleep.

ideas.title: gh CLI
ideas.hook: Manage GitHub without ever opening a browser.
ideas.script: The gh CLI brings all of GitHub to your terminal. Create repos, open pull requests, and review issues by command. Install it with winget install GitHub.cli on Windows. On Mac run brew install gh. Run gh auth login once to connect your account. Then gh repo create makes a repo in seconds. Use gh pr create to open a PR from your branch. Your whole workflow without leaving the keyboard.
ideas.publisher: winget install GitHub.cli / brew install gh

ideas.title: Sim Studio agent builder
ideas.hook: Sim Studio is a clean canvas for wiring up AI agents fast.
ideas.script: Sim Studio is open source for building agentic workflows. Clone the repo and start it with the provided docker setup. Open the editor and drag an Agent block onto the board. Connect tool blocks like web search or an API call. Set the model and the system prompt in the side panel. Run it and watch the agent route through your blocks. It feels like a flowchart that actually thinks.

ideas.title: Open Interpreter runs your computer
ideas.hook: I let an AI control my entire computer with one terminal command.
ideas.script: Run pip install open-interpreter in your terminal. Type interpreter to start the session. Set your model with the dash dash model flag. Ask it to rename every file in a folder by date. It writes the code, shows you the plan, and asks to run it. Approve it and watch your files reorganize themselves. It can edit photos, scrape sites, and control apps. ChatGPT that actually does things on your machine.

ideas.title: jq (JSON)
ideas.hook: Stop squinting at raw JSON. This slices it instantly.
ideas.script: jq is the standard tool for parsing JSON in the terminal. Filter, reshape, and pretty print any JSON stream. Install it with winget install jqlang.jq on Windows. On Mac run brew install jq. Pipe an API response into jq dot for clean color output. Use jq dot items to drill into any field. Chain filters to extract exactly what you need. JSON finally bends to your will.
ideas.publisher: winget install jqlang.jq / brew install jq

ideas.title: Google Veo in Flow
ideas.hook: Google's video AI is now public and the quality is insane.
ideas.script: Go to labs.google/flow and sign in with your Google account. Click New Project, then the text box at the bottom. Pick Veo as the model. Type "a paper boat floating down a rainy street, cinematic." Hit generate and wait for the render. The lighting and detail look like a real camera. Export the clip directly from Flow.

ideas.title: tmux
ideas.hook: Close your terminal and your work keeps running.
ideas.script: tmux lets you split panes and keep sessions alive forever. Disconnect, walk away, and reattach later untouched. Install it with brew install tmux on Mac. On Windows run it inside WSL with sudo apt install tmux. Start with tmux and split panes using Ctrl B then a key. Detach with Ctrl B then d. Reattach anytime with tmux attach. Your sessions survive even when you do not.
ideas.publisher: brew install tmux / apt install tmux (WSL)

ideas.title: Google Sheets and Docs voice typing
ideas.hook: Google Docs can type everything you say for free.
ideas.script: Open a Google Doc and click the Tools menu. Choose Voice typing. A microphone button appears, click it and start talking. It types your words in real time. Say "period" or "new line" to add punctuation. It is shockingly accurate and totally free. Dictate a whole draft without touching the keyboard.

ideas.title: Bolt.new (prompt to full app)
ideas.hook: I typed one sentence and this site built and deployed a working app in the browser.
ideas.script: Go to bolt.new and type what you want, like "a recipe app with search and favorites." It spins up a full Node project right in your browser, no install. Watch it write files and run npm install live. The preview updates as it builds. Click any file to edit by hand or just tell Bolt to change it. Hit the Deploy button to push it live in one click. Free to start and you walk away with real code.

ideas.title: Chatbase support chatbot
ideas.hook: I trained a customer support bot on my website in five minutes.
ideas.script: Open Chatbase and paste your website URL. It crawls your pages and FAQ. The bot now answers questions in your brand voice. Test it with a few tricky questions. Tweak the tone in settings. Grab the embed code. Paste it on your site. A 24/7 support rep that never sleeps.

ideas.title: AI pitch deck with Tome
ideas.hook: Tome built my investor pitch deck from a single paragraph.
ideas.script: Open Tome and click generate. Paste a short description of your startup. AI writes and designs every slide. It adds your problem, solution, and market. Edit any slide with a text prompt. Drop in your logo and brand colors. Present right from the browser. A funding-ready deck without touching PowerPoint.

ideas.title: SuperAGI autonomous agent
ideas.hook: I deployed an autonomous AI agent with a dashboard and it runs goals on its own.
ideas.script: Clone the SuperAGI repo from GitHub. Run docker compose up to start the whole stack. Open localhost:3000 in your browser. Add your OpenAI key in the settings panel. Create a new agent and type a goal in plain English. Pick its tools like web search and file writing. Hit run and watch the live activity feed. It breaks the goal into steps and executes them itself.

ideas.title: Windsurf (AI-native editor)
ideas.hook: This editor's AI keeps working after you stop typing, here's the Cascade flow.
ideas.script: Download Windsurf from windsurf.com and open your project folder. Press Ctrl+L to open Cascade, the agent panel. Type a goal like "add dark mode toggle across the app." Cascade finds every relevant file on its own and proposes edits. Click Accept All or step through each diff. It remembers what it did so follow-ups like "now persist it" just work. Tab autocomplete predicts your next edit, not just the next word. The free tier gives you real agent runs.

ideas.title: Liner
ideas.hook: This free extension highlights the web like a textbook and pulls AI answers from your highlights.
ideas.script: Search Liner in the Chrome Web Store and click Add to Chrome. Sign in and open any article you want to study. Select text and pick a highlight color from the popup. Open your Liner dashboard to see every highlight saved by topic. Click Ask AI to summarize or question your highlighted notes. Use it on Google to surface the most trusted sources first. It syncs across your devices automatically. Free research tool that turns reading into knowledge.

ideas.title: Agno fast agents
ideas.hook: This is the fastest way to build an AI agent in pure Python, no bloat.
ideas.script: Run pip install agno in your terminal. Import Agent and a model like OpenAIChat. Create an agent in one line with a model and instructions. Add the DuckDuckGo tool so it can search the web. Call agent.print\_response with your question. It searches, reasons, and answers in seconds. Agno agents start up faster than almost anything else. Minimal code, maximum speed.

ideas.title: DuckDuckGo browser (private browsing)
ideas.hook: This browser deletes your trackers and your history with one tap.
ideas.script: Download the DuckDuckGo browser from duckduckgo.com. Set it as your default when it asks. Browse normally and watch the tracker counter climb as it blocks them. Tap the fire button to torch all tabs and data instantly. Turn on Email Protection to get a free duck.com alias. Use App Tracking Protection on Android to stop apps phoning home. Search is private by default with no profile built on you. Free, fast, no account.

ideas.title: Mendeley
ideas.hook: This free tool stores every paper you read and cites them for you in Word.
ideas.script: Download Mendeley and create a free account. Drag your PDFs into the library and it pulls the details automatically. Highlight and annotate papers right inside the reader. Install the Word plugin to cite as you write. Pick any citation style and switch it in one click. Sync across devices so your library follows you. Never lose a source or fumble a citation again.

ideas.title: Claude Code with MCP servers
ideas.hook: I connected my coding agent to my database and it queried it on its own.
ideas.script: In your terminal, run claude mcp add to register a server. Add the Playwright or filesystem MCP with the one-line command from the docs. Now in Claude Code, ask "check the live page for the broken link." It uses the connected tool to actually browse and report back. Add a Postgres MCP and it can read your real schema. Each tool extends what the agent can do beyond code. List them anytime with /mcp inside the session. Your agent gets superpowers from real tools.

ideas.title: AI YouTube script writer with VidIQ
ideas.hook: This tool writes your next video script from the title alone.
ideas.script: Open VidIQ and go to the AI tools. Click Script Generator. Paste your video title. Pick the length and tone. It writes a hook, body, and outro. Edit the sections to fit your voice. Record straight from the script.

ideas.title: Canva Magic Design
ideas.hook: Upload one photo and Canva designs a whole template set for you.
ideas.script: Open Canva and find Magic Design on the homepage. Upload a product photo or type a prompt. It returns dozens of matching templates instantly. Click one to open it in the editor. Use Magic Switch to resize it for any platform. Apply your brand kit colors with one tap. Download the pack when you like it. A full branded set generated from a single image.

ideas.title: chocolatey
ideas.hook: The original Windows package manager still rules for one thing.
ideas.script: chocolatey installs apps system wide with full automation. Great for software that needs admin and machine wide setup. Install it with one PowerShell line from chocolatey.org. Then choco install with the package name. Use choco upgrade all to update your whole system. It has the largest Windows package catalog around. Mass install your dev stack in one go.
